title: Tenant Renewal Brief
description: Prepare a renewal or expiry brief for a commercial tenancy covering lease options, tenant risk, service history, market context, and recommended next actions.
whyItMatters: Renewal preparation is where operational knowledge meets leasing strategy. A good brief helps the owner make timely decisions on retention, incentives, refurbishment, and vacancy risk instead of waiting until the date pressure becomes expensive.
skillsLearned: Combining market context with service history
Translating tenant experience into occupancy strategy

steps.title: Abstract the lease position
steps.description: Capture key dates, options, notice windows, make-good clauses, current rental terms, incentives, and any outstanding obligations that could affect renewal discussions.
steps.tips: Never talk strategy before you are certain on the lease mechanics.

steps.title: Assess the relationship and risk
steps.description: Summarize the tenant's payment history, service issues, complaint pattern, fitout condition, and recent business signals that may affect renewal likelihood.
steps.tips: Retention risk is often visible in behavior before it is visible in a formal notice.

steps.title: Add market and asset context
steps.description: Bring in comparable asking rents, vacancy conditions, incentives, and any competing space or upcoming capex that could affect the owner's decision.
steps.tips: A renewal brief should help the owner compare retention to reletting, not just summarize the lease.

steps.title: Write the recommendation
steps.description: Present a recommended path: pursue renewal, negotiate early, prepare backfill strategy, or escalate for legal or asset-management input.
steps.tips: Keep the recommendation practical and time-bound.

deliverables: A renewal recommendation memo with next steps

rubric.description: The brief captures the right dates, options, and obligations without ambiguity.
The recommendation balances tenant relationship, market conditions, and owner value logically.
The brief surfaces meaningful renewal and vacancy risks instead of assuming continuation.
A leasing or asset team could use the brief immediately to prepare next actions.
